title: Finding Your Way Around Melbourne in Week One
summary: Grid, spine streets, and trams as a compass so the inner suburbs start to feel legible.

sections.title: The Moment
sections.value: Day four. A fifteen-minute walk turns into thirty because a one-way street and a tram line pointed the wrong way.

sections.title: The Feeling
sections.value: The city is not hostile — it is just written in a dialect you have not learned yet.

sections.title: The Reveal
sections.value: The CBD is a tight grid; just outside, streets curve. Learn four spine streets and the tram lines that follow them, and most inner suburbs click into place.

sections.title: How It Actually Works
sections.value: Use Swanston, Elizabeth, Flinders and Collins as anchors. Toggle walking vs transit in maps deliberately. Treat trams as north-south or east-west backbones. Repeat the same commute twice — familiarity beats novelty this week.

sections.title: The Bridge
sections.value: When you are ready, find stops, bike share and paths near you.

sections.title: Next Chapter
sections.value: Next: Making Friends in a City Where Everyone's Busy — turning place into people.
